title: Saturator
summary: Adds harmonic distortion for warmth and translation. Used on sub bass so it's audible on small speakers, and on pads to add character. More precise than Overdrive for controlled harmonic shaping.
steps: Add Saturator to a sub bass or pad track
Choose a waveshaper mode: Soft Clip = gentle, warm saturation. Hard Clip = more aggressive, squared-off distortion. Analog Clip = SSL-style colour
For sub bass: Drive 15%, Soft Clip mode, Dry/Wet 40% — adds 2nd and 3rd harmonics so the bass is audible on headphones and small speakers
For a chord pad: Drive 10%, Soft Clip, Dry/Wet 25% — removes the 'too digital' quality from a Serum patch
For a master bus or drum bus: Drive 5%, Analog Clip, Dry/Wet 20% — adds subtle tape-like warmth
The Waveshaper display shows the distortion curve — watch how Soft Clip curves gently vs Hard Clip squaring off
tips: Saturator on sub bass is not about making it distorted — it's about adding harmonics that translate on systems that can't reproduce deep sub frequencies
Test sub bass saturation on earbuds or laptop speakers: with Saturator off, the bass disappears. With it on, you should hear the character of the bass even on small speakers
Dry/Wet knob is key — 100% is full saturation. 20–40% blends the saturated version with the original for controlled warmth
Unlike Overdrive, Saturator lets you see exactly what the distortion curve looks like — use the Waveshaper display to understand how your settings affect the waveform

images.caption: Set Drive to 15% for sub bass or 10% for a pad. Select Soft Clip mode using the shape selector (the gentle curve icon). Set Dry/Wet to 40% to blend saturation with the dry signal. Toggle the device on/off while the track plays — the bass should feel warmer and more present with it on.

ableton_version_note: Available in all editions of Live 9+.
